explanation:
Personal finance and investing use universal mathematical principles (compound interest, time value of money, risk-return optimization). This calculator applies standard formulas from the financial industry.

## How It's Calculated

Annual bonus: calculating income tax withholding on your bonus payment.

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting when values seem outside normal bounds.

## Typical Benchmark Returns by Time Horizon

| Time Horizon | S&P 500 | 10-Year Bonds | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4–5% | 3–5% |
| 5 years | ~8% average | ~4% | Inflation |
| 10 years | ~9–10% | ~4% | <inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Investment Principles

- **Stick to the plan** during market downturns. Emotion is the worst advisor.
- **Diversify**: don't concentrate in one asset, sector, or country.
- **Keep costs low**: fees >1% annually eat 30% of your returns over 30 years.
- **Use a long time horizon**: stocks work over 10+ years, not 1 year.
- **Rebalance**: once per year, no more.

## Final Notes

This calculation is for reference only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Values are accurate for 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: Is my annual bonus subject to income tax?
faq.a: Yes, bonuses are taxed as ordinary income in the year you receive them, added to your total gross income.

faq.q: What withholding rate applies to bonuses?
faq.a: Federal withholding is typically 22% for bonuses up to $1 million and 37% for amounts exceeding $1 million. Your actual rate depends on your total annual income and tax bracket.

faq.q: How is my net bonus calculated?
faq.a: Net bonus = gross bonus × (1 − withholding rate). The calculator applies your specified tax rate to determine net pay.

faq.q: Does my bonus push me into a higher tax bracket?
faq.a: Potentially. Bonuses are added to your annual income, which could increase your marginal tax rate and result in more tax owed.

faq.q: What about FICA taxes (Social Security and Medicare)?
faq.a: FICA taxes (7.65% combined) also apply to bonuses in addition to income tax withholding. Your total deductions will be higher than income tax alone.

faq.q: Can I adjust my W-4 when I receive a bonus?
faq.a: Yes, you can adjust your W-4 to increase or decrease withholding to better match your expected annual tax liability.

faq.q: Should I expect additional taxes at tax time?
faq.a: Possibly. If your actual tax rate exceeds the withholding rate, you may owe additional taxes when filing your return, or you may receive a refund.

faq.q: How much can I expect to earn long-term from investments?
faq.a: S&P 500 historical average: ~10% nominal annual return (7% real, adjusted for inflation). Past performance doesn't predict future results, but it's a useful reference.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology
